meaning: adj.不劳而获的,自然得到的,不相称的,不应得的
examples: Unearned fees are money a business collects from a customer up front for services the company has yet to perform.
The abolition of incomes unearned by work.
The worst guilt is to accept an unearned guilt.
The proposed regulations would cause them to pay Medicare tax on phantom unearned income.
This is unearned revenue that represents software services that are still owed to clients.
